papers.title: Microstructural Evolution and Mechanical Properties of Nb-Containing Medium-Mn Low-Density Steels
papers.paper_scope: Investigating the effect of Nb microalloying (0.02 and 0.04 wt% Nb) on microstructure evolution, nano-scale precipitation (NbC, κ-carbide), and mechanical properties of Fe-12Mn-9Al-3Cr-1.4C medium-Mn low-density steels, with quantitative strengthening analysis and thermodynamic/DFT calculations.

alloys.alloys_notes: Actual measured composition (wt%): Mn 12.80, Al 9.01, Cr 3.01, C 1.39, Nb 0.021, Fe Bal.
Actual measured composition (wt%): Mn 12.82, Al 9.12, Cr 2.99, C 1.40, Nb 0.039, Fe Bal.

processes.description: Preparation of steel strips by centrifugal casting under near-rapid solidification: induction melting in water-cooled copper crucible, re-melting and pouring into rotating copper mold to obtain strips of 60×70×2.5 mm.

processing_steps.processing_steps_notes: Aged in a tubular vacuum furnace.

Aged in a tubular vacuum furnace.

structures.overall_structure: Ferrite-austenite dual-phase structure, with irregular-shaped δ-ferrite dispersed in γ-austenite matrix.

grain_structure.grain_size_distribution: 0.02Nb steel as-cast austenite grain size not directly measured, but Nb addition expected to refine grains.

precipitates.distribution: Submicron white particles within austenite matrix (identified by TEM).

structures.overall_structure: Ferrite-austenite dual-phase structure with NbC particles and intragranular κ-carbides; ferrite content ≈16.4 vol%.

grain_structure.grain_size_distribution: 0.02Nb steel: austenite grain size larger than 0.04Nb variant. NbC particles refine grain.

precipitates.distribution: Slightly coarsened submicron particles in austenite.
Intragranular, no coarsened intergranular κ-carbides detected.

structures.overall_structure: Ferrite-austenite dual-phase structure with NbC and κ-carbides; ferrite content ≈17.1 vol%.

grain_structure.grain_size_distribution: Austenite grain size remains larger than 0.04Nb counterpart.

precipitates.distribution: Intragranular, no coarsened intergranular κ-carbides detected. Larger than those in 0.04Nb steel.

structures.overall_structure: Ferrite-austenite dual-phase structure with NbC and κ-carbides; ferrite content ≈18.3 vol%.

grain_structure.grain_size_distribution: Austenite grain size larger than 0.04Nb variant.

precipitates.distribution: Intragranular, no coarsened intergranular κ-carbides detected.

structures.overall_structure: Ferrite-austenite dual-phase structure; irregular-shaped δ-ferrite dispersed in austenite matrix.

grain_structure.grain_size_distribution: Austenite grain size about half that of 0.02Nb steel due to NbC precipitation.

precipitates.distribution: Submicron white particles in austenite (TEM identified).

structures.overall_structure: Ferrite-austenite dual-phase structure with NbC and fine κ-carbides; ferrite content ≈17.2 vol%.

grain_structure.grain_size_distribution: Grain refined by NbC, about half of 0.02Nb variant.

precipitates.distribution: Intragranular, consistently smaller than in 0.02Nb steel; no coarsened intergranular κ-carbides.

structures.overall_structure: Ferrite-austenite dual-phase structure with NbC and very fine κ-carbides; ferrite content ≈18.4 vol%.

grain_structure.grain_size_distribution: Grain refined by NbC, about half that of 0.02Nb steel.

precipitates.distribution: Intragranular, smaller than in 0.02Nb steel; no coarsened intergranular κ-carbides.

structures.overall_structure: Ferrite-austenite dual-phase structure with NbC and fine κ-carbides; ferrite content ≈18.1 vol%.

grain_structure.grain_size_distribution: Grain refined by NbC, about half that of 0.02Nb variant.

precipitates.distribution: Slightly larger than at lower aging temperatures.
Intragranular, smaller than 0.02Nb steel; no coarsened intergranular κ-carbides detected.

characterization_methods.purpose: Microstructure observation and ferrite content measurement
characterization_methods.key_findings: Irregular δ-ferrite dispersed in austenite, ferrite content ~16.2 vol%.

characterization_methods.purpose: Grain orientation and grain size analysis
characterization_methods.key_findings: Austenite grain size larger than 0.04Nb steel.
SAED and EDS confirmed submicron NbC particles.
Phases remain γ + δ; no new phases.

characterization_methods.purpose: Microstructure observation, ferrite content and NbC particle analysis
characterization_methods.key_findings: Ferrite content ~17.1 vol%; NbC particles slightly coarsened.
Austenite grain size larger than 0.04Nb counterpart.
Intragranular κ-carbides present; no coarsened intergranular κ-carbides detected.
Irregular δ-ferrite dispersed in austenite, ferrite content ~16.3 vol%.
Austenite grain size about half of 0.02Nb steel due to NbC precipitation.
SAED and EDS confirmed submicron NbC particles present.
γ + δ phases; no significant change in peak intensity.
Ferrite content ~18.4 vol%; NbC particles visible.
Austenite grain size about half of 0.02Nb variant.
Intragranular κ-carbides consistently smaller than in 0.02Nb steel; no coarsened intergranular κ-carbides.

computational_details.potential_model: PAW-PBE with U scheme (U=2.59 eV for Fe), Gaussian smearing 0.05 eV

analytical_model.model_note: DFT calculation used to evaluate the formation energy of κ-carbide and the effect of Nb addition on it.
Thermodynamic calculations performed to explain precipitation behavior of κ-carbide and NbC; details of software not specified.

unmapped_findings: The yield strength of 0.04Nb steels exceeds that of 0.02Nb steels by ≈71 MPa, attributed to additional fine grain strengthening and dislocation strengthening despite a slight reduction in κ-carbide precipitation strengthening.
Austenite grain size of 0.04Nb steels is approximately half that of 0.02Nb steels due to NbC precipitation.
Nb addition elevates the formation energy of κ-carbide, impeding its growth and coarsening; no coarsened intergranular κ-carbides were detected in either steel after aging at 723–823 K.
Ferrite content is not significantly affected by aging temperature or Nb content: as-cast 0.02Nb ~16.2 vol%, aged up to 18.3 vol% at 823 K; 0.04Nb similar.
XRD patterns show no significant change in austenite/ferrite peak intensities with aging.

Microhardness and tensile properties were tested but quantitative values (except the 71 MPa difference) are not provided in the available text; further mechanical data may be present in the full paper.

NbC particles exist as submicron white particles within the austenite matrix; their size and volume fraction increase slightly with higher aging temperature and Nb content according to Figure 4 statistics (numerical values not extracted from image).
The paper includes Figure 1 (XRD), Figure 2 (SEM), Figure 3 (TEM of NbC), Figure 4 (NbC statistics), and Figure 5 (EBSD maps); details from images are not fully captured as pure text mode.
Strain rate for tensile tests was 2.5 × 10^-4 s^-1; specimens had gauge width 4 mm and gauge length 12 mm; each test repeated at least four times.

Aging treatments were performed at 723, 773, 823 K for 3 h in a tubular vacuum furnace; the appropriate aging period for further investigation was selected but not explicitly stated in the provided text.
Electropolishing parameters: 10 vol% perchloric acid alcohol solution, 25 V, 10 s for SEM; TEM sample preparation: 10% perchloric acid + 90% alcohol, -30 °C, 35 V.
